title: FORMAT_DSP_TEMPLATE (Obsolete)
description: Parses and formats a DSP template string by resolving embedded expressions and placeholders.

descriptionCallouts.content: Obsolete since v3.418. Use FORMAT_STRING_TEMPLATE with merged fields in the new {!expression} syntax. Existing invocations continue to work for backward compatibility.

parameters.name: template
parameters.description: A string containing merge fields or expressions in DSP template syntax (e.g. {!FieldName}, {VLOOKUP(...)}) to be evaluated and replaced with their corresponding values. Incorrect or unparseable expressions remain literal strings in the output.

examples:
FORMAT_DSP_TEMPLATE(
'Dear <shadow>{VLOOKUP("OpportunityContactRole", "Contact.Name", "OpportunityId", Id,"IsPrimary = TRUE")}</shadow>, 

We are pleased to inform you that your Account: <shadow>{Account.Name}</shadow> has an Opportunity currently at the stage: <shadow>{StageName}</shadow>.

Feel free to reach out if you have any questions.

Best Regards,   
Sales Team')

/*Dear <shadow>Kristin Stanton</shadow>, 

We are pleased to inform you that your Account: <shadow>Owens LLC</shadow> has an Opportunity currently at the stage: <shadow>Prospecting</shadow>. 

Feel free to reach out if you have any questions. 

Best Regards, 
Sales Team*/

FORMAT_DSP_TEMPLATE('Hello <shadow>{LOOKUP("Account", "Name", "Id", AccountId)}</shadow>. Your Stage: <shadow>{Opty.StageName}</shadow>.')
-- Returns the template string with unparseable expressions unchanged because "LOOKUP" is misspelled and "Opty" is an invalid object name.

tips.text: To validate embedded expressions, test them individually in a field mapping's editor.
